content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcer- PRACrrcBs CoMMrssroN
428 J Street e Suire 620 r Sacramenro, CA 95gl.l_2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
This letter was SUPERSEDED by lhe Brown Advice Letter, No. A-o9-276
June 19,2008
Stephen J. Kaufman
Kaufman Downing LLP
777 S. Figueron Street, Suite 4050
Los Angeles, CA 90017
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-06-106
Dear Mr. Kaufman:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of State Senator Kevin
Murray,regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act"). 
1
QrrEsTroN
May Senator Murray transfer, with attribution, contributions he raised for
the 2006 general election to his campaign committee established for the 2010
election to the office of State Treasurer?2
CONCLUSION
No. Because Senator Murray's withdrawal from the 2006 primary
election for the office of the State Treasurer effectuated a withdrawal from the
general election, funds he raised for the general election to that office must be
returned to contributors.
FACTS
Senator Munay was a 2006 candidate for the office of State Treasurer. He
raised contributions into his controlled committee, Elect Kevin Murray
I Government Code sections S lUn - grc14. Commission regulations appear atTitle 2,
sections 18109- 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 You have not asked about use of campaig-n funds raised by the Senator fbr the 2006
primary election and we do not opine on the use of such funds.


File No. ,4.-06-106
Page No.2
committee (D# 1253089), for both the 2006 pimary and general elections as
permitted by law. Senator Murray ultimately decided not to seek the office of
Treasurer during the2006 election cyclg and did not file the nomination papers
required to appeu on the 2006 pimary election ballot. He has instead opt"a to
run for State Treasurer in 2010, and has opened a committee in connection with
the 2010 election, Elect Kevin Murray Committee 2olo, (Fppc rD#12g36g6).
Senator Murray has funds remaining in his 2006 committee that were designated
for both the primary and general elections.
ANALYSIs
Primary and General Elections: Section 82I22defines an "election" as
"any pimary, general, special or recall election held in this state. The primary
and general or special elections are separate elections for purposes of this title.,,
Thus, contribution limits apply separately to primary and general elections
(sections 85301 and 85302), even.though the prima{y and general elections are for
the same office. Thus, two different contributions rip to the applicable limits (one
for the primary election and one for the general election) are permitted from each
contributor. And when reporting conkibutions received, "*didutrs 
are required
to specifically identiff whether the contribution was for the primary or general
, election. (Regulation 18421.4.)
Timtng: Section 85318 allows "a candidate for elective state office,, to
accqt contributions towards the general election prior to the primary election. As
the office of the State Treasurer is an elective state office under.".tior, g2024,
Senator Murray was able to raise funds into a general election account or
committee prior to the conclusion of the primary election n2006.
Defeat or Withdrawal: However, section 85318 f,rtherprovides: .,Ifthe
candidate for elective state office is defeated in the primary eleciion or special
primary election, or otherwise withdraws from the generaf election or special
general election, the general election or special general election funds slhal be
refunded to the contributors on a pro rata basis llss any expenses associated with
the raising and administration of general election or speciai general election
contributions."
According to your facts, Senator Murray raised contributions into his
controlled committee for both the2006 primary and general elections. Senator
Murray ultimately decided not to seek the office of Treasurer during the 2006
election cycle, and did not file the nomination papers required to aplear on the
2006 pimary eleition ballot. you ask now whether the senator may use the
funds raised specifically for the 2006 general election in future elections.


File No. A-06-106
Page No. 3
You note that the withdrawal occurred prior to the pimary. since
winning the primary is a necessary prerequisite in order to be on the ballot for the
general election, the Senator's withdrawal from the primary election effecfuates a
withdrawal from the general election for State Treasurer in2006 as well. (See
e.g., Election Code section 13.) Therefore, we conclude that Senator Murray
withdrew from the 2006 general election and the general election funds -.6t b.
retumed to contributors consistent with the provisions of section g53 l g.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

sections.conclusion:
No. Because Senator Murray's withdrawal from the 2006 primary
election for the office of the State Treasurer effectuated a withdrawal from the
general election, funds he raised for the general election to that office must be
returned to contributors.

embedding.qa_text:
CONCLUSION: No. Because Senator Murray's withdrawal from the 2006 primary
election for the office of the State Treasurer effectuated a withdrawal from the
general election, funds he raised for the general election to that office must be
returned to contributors.
